title: Peak Index in Mountain Array
statement:
An array `nums` is a **mountain array** if:
- It has at least 3 elements
- There exists some index `i` where `nums[0] < nums[1] < ... < nums[i] > nums[i+1] > ... > nums[n-1]`

Given a mountain array, return the index of the peak element using `O(log n)` time.

**Input:** Space-separated integers forming a mountain array.
**Output:** Print the 0-based index of the peak.
constraints:
- 3 ≤ nums.length ≤ 10⁵
- 0 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10⁶
- nums is guaranteed to be a mountain array.
